title: Catamaran Charter in Bali: Costs, Seasons and Tips 2026
html:
A catamaran charter in Bali means a stable, shaded day on calm tropical water, usually starting from Benoa Harbour or Serangan, with a private day boat from roughly IDR 8,000,000. BoatHire24 lists skippered catamarans across Bali so you can match guests, route and budget.

Where to set off in Bali?

Most catamaran charters leave from the south coast, near Benoa Harbour and Serangan. Each departure point suits a different day on the water.

  • Benoa Harbour: the main deep-water marina, used by larger catamarans and crewed boats.
  • Serangan (Turtle Island): a popular launch for trips toward Nusa Penida.
  • Sanur: a calm east-coast town and the classic gateway to the Nusa islands.
  • Padang Bai: an eastern port handy for Blue Lagoon snorkelling and Lombok crossings.
  • Amed: a quieter north-east base known for calm bays and clear water.

Nusa Lembongan sits roughly 12 nautical miles off Sanur, a comfortable day-charter target.

Which boat to rent in Bali?

For most groups the right choice is a skippered sailing catamaran, which trades top speed for shade, deck space and a steady platform. Consider the line-up below before you book.

  1. Sailing catamaran: best for island-hopping and snorkelling days, seats around 10 to 12 guests, from IDR 12,000,000. The Sanyati Leopard 51 shows this class well, and you can study its layout on this catamaran before deciding.
  2. Power catamaran: best for reaching Nusa Penida quickly, seats around 12 guests, from IDR 15,000,000.
  3. Motorboat or speedboat: best for short coastal hops, seats up to 8 guests, from IDR 6,000,000.
  4. Phinisi-style wooden yacht: best for overnight cruising and diving, sleeps 8 to 10 guests, from IDR 25,000,000 per day.
  5. Crewed luxury yacht: best for special occasions, seats 10 to 12 guests, from IDR 30,000,000.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran in Bali?

Expect roughly IDR 8,000,000 to IDR 30,000,000 for a private catamaran day, with shared seats far cheaper. Prices in 2026 move with season and boat size, so the surest way to gauge real cost is to find a skippered charter for your dates and compare quotes.

  • Shared group day tour to the Nusa islands: from about IDR 750,000 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day charter: from about IDR 4,000,000 for the boat.
  • Catamaran full-day private charter: from about IDR 12,000,000 in low season.
  • Crewed luxury yacht for a full day: from about IDR 30,000,000 with skipper and crew.

How does booking a catamaran in Bali work?

You pick a boat and date, send your guest count and route, and the operator confirms price and meeting point before you pay. Bali departure times shift with the tide, so a flexible start hour helps.

If you are new to chartering, it helps to read how the process runs, from deposits to skipper handover, and the operator confirms pick-up details for your marina.

When is the best season to charter in Bali?

The dry season from May to September is the prime window, with calmer seas and reliable sunshine. Bali sits in the tropics, so the water stays warm year round, but the wet season from November to March brings heavier showers and occasional swell.

Local tip: book your catamaran for a morning start during the dry season, when the south-easterly trade wind, known locally as the Angin Tenggara, keeps the water flat off Sanur and Nusa Lembongan before the afternoon chop builds.

On licensing, Indonesia treats commercial passenger trips as the operator's responsibility, so a skippered catamaran needs no qualification from you. The operator confirms exact crew and permit requirements.

At a glance

  • Typical price band: IDR 8,000,000 to IDR 30,000,000 per private day.
  • Peak months: May to September.
  • Trip length: a full day runs around 7 to 8 hours.
  • Capacity: most catamarans take 10 to 12 guests.
  • Currency: Indonesian rupiah, IDR.
  • Main departure: Benoa Harbour and Serangan in south Bali.

Questions about boat rental in Bali

Do I need a licence to charter a catamaran in Bali?

You do not need a personal boating licence when you book a skippered catamaran, because a qualified local crew handles the boat. The operator holds the necessary permits and confirms the exact requirements for your chosen route.

How many people fit on a Bali catamaran?

Most catamarans in Bali comfortably carry between 10 and 12 guests for a day trip, with shaded seating. Larger crewed yachts can take more, so share your group size when you request a quote.

How long is a typical catamaran day trip?

A standard private day charter in Bali lasts about 7 to 8 hours, usually a morning departure with time to snorkel, swim and reach an island such as Nusa Lembongan before late afternoon.

What should I bring on a Bali charter?

Bring reef-safe sun cream, a hat, a light layer for the breeze and any snorkelling gear you prefer, although most operators supply masks and fins. Cash in IDR is useful for small extras ashore.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Bali or list your own. To see what is free for your dates, browse the listings and request a quote for your group. Boat owners along Bali's coast can also add their vessel and reach travellers looking to charter.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Bali and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
